ucs.title: [LLM] Sowbug (G0054) Felismus loader execution from masqueraded Windows lookalike directories
ucs.description: Hunts for executables running from fake/duplicated Windows system folders (e.g., C:\Windows\Help\Help\, C:\Windows\Cursors\Cursors\) — a signature staging pattern Symantec attributed to Sowbug's Felismus RAT, where the operator hides binaries in nested directories that look like legitimate OS paths and uses filenames like adobecms.exe / fb.exe to blend in.
ucs.splunk: | tstats summariesonly=t count min(_time) as firstTime max(_time) as lastTime values(Processes.process) as cmdlines values(Processes.parent_process_name) as parents from datamodel=Endpoint.Processes where (Processes.process_path IN ("*\\Windows\\Help\\Help\\*","*\\Windows\\Cursors\\Cursors\\*","*\\Windows\\Fonts\\Fonts\\*","*\\Windows\\System\\System\\*","*\\Windows\\Tasks\\Tasks\\*","*\\Intel\\Intel\\*") OR (Processes.process_name IN ("adobecms.exe","fb.exe","msvcrt.exe","ntmssvc.exe","alg.exe","lsass.exe","svchost.exe") AND NOT Processes.process_path IN ("*\\System32\\*","*\\SysWOW64\\*","*\\WinSxS\\*"))) by Processes.dest Processes.user Processes.process_name Processes.process_path Processes.process Processes.parent_process_name | `drop_dm_object_name(Processes)` | `security_content_ctime(firstTime)`
ucs.kql:
DeviceProcessEvents
| where Timestamp > ago(30d)
| extend LowerPath = tolower(FolderPath)
| where LowerPath matches regex @"\\windows\\(help\\help|cursors\\cursors|fonts\\fonts|tasks\\tasks|system\\system)\\"
   or LowerPath matches regex @"\\(intel|nvidia|amd)\\\1\\"
   or (FileName in~ ("adobecms.exe","fb.exe","msvcrt.exe","ntmssvc.exe","alg.exe","svchost.exe","lsass.exe")
       and LowerPath !startswith @"c:\windows\system32"
       and LowerPath !startswith @"c:\windows\syswow64"
       and LowerPath !startswith @"c:\windows\winsxs")
| project Timestamp, DeviceName, AccountName, FileName, FolderPath, ProcessCommandLine, InitiatingProcessFileName, InitiatingProcessFolderPath, SHA256
| join kind=leftouter (DeviceFileEvents | where ActionType == "FileCreated" | project SHA256, FileOriginUrl, InitiatingProcessAccountName) on SHA256
ucs.rationale: Sowbug's Felismus deployments documented by Symantec consistently used doubled directory names under C:\Windows\ (e.g., Help\Help, Cursors\Cursors) as a masquerading trick — this nested-twin-directory pattern is rare in benign software and is a near-fingerprint for this actor. Combined with binaries that impersonate System32 names but execute from non-System32 paths, this catches the specific stag

ucs.title: [LLM] Sowbug remote share document harvesting chained to WinRAR staging
ucs.description: Detects the actor's signature collection chain: enumeration of network shares with built-in commands (net view / dir \\), targeted listing of Office documents on remote UNC paths, followed within hours by WinRAR archiving with a password — the exact TTP Sowbug used to steal foreign-affairs documents from Latin American and Southeast Asian government networks.
ucs.splunk: | tstats summariesonly=t count min(_time) as firstTime max(_time) as lastTime values(Processes.process) as cmdlines values(Processes.process_name) as procs from datamodel=Endpoint.Processes where (Processes.process IN ("*net view*","*net use \\\\*","*dir \\\\*","*net.exe view*","*net share*") OR (Processes.process_name IN ("rar.exe","winrar.exe","WinRAR.exe","Rar.exe") AND Processes.process IN ("* a -hp*","* a -r *","*.docx*","*.xlsx*","*.pdf*","*.pptx*"))) by Processes.dest Processes.user _time span=4h | `drop_dm_object_name(Processes)` | eval has_share_enum=if(match(mvjoin(cmdlines,"|"),"(?i)(net view|net use \\\\\\\\|dir \\\\\\\\|net share)"),1,0) | eval has_rar_docs=if(match(mvjoin(cmdlines,"|"),"(?i)(rar(\.exe)?.*( a -hp| a -r ).*(\.docx|\.xlsx|\.pdf|\.pptx))"),1,0) | where has_share_enum=1 AND has_rar_docs=1 | `security_content_ctime(firstTime)`
ucs.kql:
let window = 6h;
let enum = DeviceProcessEvents
| where Timestamp > ago(14d)
| where (FileName =~ "net.exe" and ProcessCommandLine has_any ("view", "share", "use \\\\"))
     or (FileName =~ "cmd.exe" and ProcessCommandLine matches regex @"(?i)dir\s+\\\\[a-z0-9._-]+\\")
| project EnumTime = Timestamp, DeviceId, DeviceName, AccountName, EnumCmd = ProcessCommandLine;
let rar = DeviceProcessEvents
| where Timestamp > ago(14d)
| where FileName in~ ("rar.exe","winrar.exe","Rar.exe")
     or ProcessCommandLine matches regex @"(?i)\brar(\.exe)?\s+a\s+(-hp|-r|-m\d|-v)"
| where ProcessCommandLine has_any (".docx",".xlsx",".pdf",".pptx",".doc",".xls")
     or ProcessCommandLine has_any ("-hp", "-r0")
| project RarTime = Timestamp, DeviceId, RarCmd = ProcessCommandLine, RarUser = AccountName, RarSHA = SHA256;
enum
| join kind=inner rar on DeviceId
| where RarTime between (EnumTime .. (EnumTime + window))
| project EnumTime, RarTime, DeviceName, AccountName, EnumCmd, RarCmd, RarSHA
| summarize EnumActions = make_set(EnumCmd, 10), RarActions = make_set(RarCmd, 10) by DeviceName, AccountName, bin(EnumTime, 1d)
ucs.rationale: Symantec's Sowbug analysis specifically documented the operator running net view / dir against UNC paths to inventory diplomatic file shares, then using a renamed/standalone WinRAR (often with -hp password flag) to archive only Office documents matching policy keywords before exfil. The temporal chain of share-enum → password-protected RAR of .docx/.xlsx within hours, on the same host/user, is hig
